title: caltha arctica subsp. membranacea
intro: Caltha arctica subsp. membranacea, also known as Arctic marsh-marigold, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Caltha arctica subsp. membranacea include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, it is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal properties and health benefits.
